📣 What's New
📝 Items requested this week
🔍
Parts ordered in a previous week and not yet received. When a shipment lands — even late, or mixed across several orders — mark what arrived. Admins can ignore items that can't be verified.

🔍
Reset History — Fleet 6

🔐 Administrative Controls

ℹ️ How the weekly cycle works: Click 📤 Submit on a part once you've ordered it in Oracle (marked Submitted so everyone can see it's handled). Every Monday at 8:00 AM the whole week's order moves to the Parts Received tab. As parts arrive, anyone marks what came in there and hits Submit Receipt (due Friday); whatever isn't received stays as awaiting fulfillment so it isn't ordered again by mistake.
Current Week's Order
Click 📤 Submit as you order each item in Oracle. Notes on not-received items are edited on the Current Weekly Order tab.

🗓️ Weekly Schedule

Set when the weekly order resets and when the Parts Received Audit is due. Applies to everyone in this fleet — change these when deploying to a fleet that runs on a different weekly cycle.
Fleet Sets “Fleet #” everywhere in the app.
Preview ⚡Fleet 6
Shown next to the fleet name on the sign-in screen. Doesn’t change department icons.

📣 Broadcast Banner

Push a scrolling message to everyone on every crew and department. It overrides the default “What’s New” ticker until it expires or you stop it.

📣 BROADCAST
Your message preview…

📤 Export Current Order

Export the current week's Fleet 6 parts order for printing or saving. Includes all departments, quantities, Oracle & SAGE numbers.

📦 Master Parts List Management

Upload New Oracle Extract (.xlsx)
No file selected
Pick a file and you'll see a preview where you can verify columns and choose how to apply: Merge (default — adds new + updates existing), Add only, Update only, or Replace. Layout doesn't have to be exact — auto-detection handles different column orders, header names ("Item Number" / "Part Number" / "P/N" etc.), and multi-sheet workbooks.
Manually Add a Part
Search, Edit & Delete Parts
🔍
Reset to the original baked-in Oracle extract

🗑 Remove items not received

Check the not received from previous week items you want to permanently remove. This week's fresh requests are not shown — they're untouched.

Use this for items the team no longer needs. If a part actually arrived, mark it received in the Parts Received Audit instead.

🚫 Ignore — no record

Use when a part can't be verified as received — no BOL or paperwork. It leaves the awaiting list and is logged, but is not counted as received. Admin only.

➕ Log a receipt

Record a part that physically arrived but isn't in the awaiting list. Search the catalog, pick it, and enter how many came in.

Order history

📺 Walkthrough

✏️ Edit Part

Update this part's details. Changes sync to everyone. Changing the Oracle # automatically carries over any orders, history and favorites to the new number.

Datavan Department

⚠️ Heads Up Datavan supplies (paper plates, utensils, cleaning products, Lysol, glass cleaner, etc.) still need to be entered separately through the Teams Request Page.

This app covers parts orders only. Make sure to submit your supplies request through Teams before your weekly deadline.
📦

Submit received parts?

—
✓ Confirm accuracy This records these parts as received for the whole fleet. There is no self-serve undo.

Submitted something by mistake? Contact your EFS (Electrical Field Supervisor) or Service Supervisor — they can return the status from the History tab.
❓

What-if scenarios

Made a mistake? Find your case and the fix.
📤Submitted a part by mistake (not received yet)▼
On the Current Weekly Order, open the item's status control and click ↩ Revert to set it back to ● Pending. This is an admin (EFS / SS) control.
📦Marked a part received by mistake▼
If you are not admin: tell your EFS or Service Supervisor.
EFS / SS: open the History tab, expand the week, and click ↩ Return status on the item. That clears the recorded receipt and puts it back on the Parts Received list to re-receive correctly.
🔢Received the wrong quantity▼
EFS / SS: History tab → ↩ Return status to clear it, then re-receive with the correct quantity on the Parts Received tab.
🧾Part arrived but the EFS hasn't ordered it in Oracle▼
Leave it ● Pending until the EFS places the Oracle order and marks it 📤 Submitted. This app mirrors Oracle — don't mark received before it's actually ordered and in hand.
🗑An item is on the order that shouldn't be▼
EFS / SS: use 🗑 Remove items… on the Admin tab to take not-received items off the order permanently.
